mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing present valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals labor market slack that reduces consumer spending growth and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the conditional mean return is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that lifts corporate revenues and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive 60-day return in > 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.04 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects capacity constraints and rising input costs that compress profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative predictive power vanishes when the macro regime shifts to early-cycle recovery, or the 20-day return correlation with INDPRO slope is positive during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 4.5 percent triggers aggressive financial condition tightening that reduces liquidity and risk appetite, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 70% of threshold-crossing events, or the conditional return distribution shows no significant left skew.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a stable or declining unemployment rate indicates labor market tightness that forces wage inflation, eroding corporate margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields a positive 20-day return in > 60% of cases, or the joint effect is statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real personal consumption expenditures directly increase corporate top-line growth and earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 2017 baseline index of 100 signals a contraction in manufacturing output that reduces economic momentum and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the conditional mean return is not significantly different from zero at the 5% level.
